# Bravo Zulu

> Bravo Zulu is a naval history research site publishing daily stories and in-depth articles about the world's warships. The name comes from the NATO signal flag code "BZ" meaning "well done." Each story starts with a surprising fact and goes deep into primary sources.

## Articles

- [The Captain Who Watched His Ship Sink From a Hotel Room](https://brvzulu.com/stories/emden-penang-raid): SMS Emden faked a British funnel, entered Penang harbor, and torpedoed the Russian cruiser Zhemchug. The Russian captain was at the Eastern & Oriental Hotel with a woman. 82 crew died. He got 3.5 years.
- [The Cruiser That Sailed Backwards With a Bow Made of Coconut Logs](https://brvzulu.com/stories/uss-new-orleans-coconut-bow): A torpedo blew off 150 feet of USS New Orleans at Tassafaronga in 1942. The crew built a coconut log bow at Tulagi and sailed backwards 1,800 miles to Sydney. The severed bow was found at 675 meters in 2025.
- [The Confederate Raider Who Captured 65 Ships and Sank in Front of French Tourists](https://brvzulu.com/stories/css-alabama-semmes): CSS Alabama spent 22 months capturing Union merchant ships. Her final battle off Cherbourg became a spectator event. Manet painted it 26 days later.
- [The Ship That Almost Lost the Unknown Soldier to the Atlantic](https://brvzulu.com/stories/uss-olympia-unknown-soldier): In 1921, the casket wouldn't fit below deck. They lashed it to the bow. USS Olympia hit a Category 4 hurricane and rolled 39 degrees.
- [The German Warship That Survived Two Nuclear Bombs and Sank From a Leak](https://brvzulu.com/stories/prinz-eugen-bikini): Prinz Eugen survived two atomic tests at Bikini Atoll but became too radioactive to repair. She sank from a small leak five months later. Her stern still sticks out at Kwajalein.

- [The Navy Welded Two Broken Destroyers Together](https://brvzulu.com/stories/hms-zubian-frankenship): HMS Zulu lost her stern, HMS Nubian lost her bow. The Royal Navy joined them into HMS Zubian. She sank a U-boat.
- [The Ship That Delivered the Bomb and Disappeared](https://brvzulu.com/stories/uss-indianapolis-sharks): USS Indianapolis delivered the Hiroshima uranium, got torpedoed, 900 men in the water for 4 days with sharks. 316 survived. Captain court-martialed, shot himself. Exonerated in 2000.
- [The Destroyer That Fired a Torpedo at the President](https://brvzulu.com/stories/uss-porter-torpedo-fdr): USS William D. Porter accidentally launched a live torpedo at FDR's ship. Then signaled the wrong warning. Ships greeted her with "Don't shoot — we're Republicans."
- [The Destroyer That Turned Left and Nobody Knows Why](https://brvzulu.com/stories/uss-hobson-wasp-collision): USS Hobson made an unexplained left turn into carrier USS Wasp during night ops. Cut in half. 176 dead in 4 minutes. Captain didn't survive to explain.
- [306 Men Sailed Out of Barbados and the Navy Never Found a Single One](https://brvzulu.com/stories/uss-cyclops-vanished): USS Cyclops with 306 aboard vanished in 1918. No SOS, no wreckage, no explanation. Largest non-combat loss in US Navy history. Still missing.
- [The Ship That Survived Pearl Harbor and Was Sunk Forty Years Later by a Torpedo Older Than Itself](https://brvzulu.com/stories/uss-phoenix-belgrano): USS Phoenix escaped Pearl Harbor, fought the entire Pacific war, was sold to Argentina, and sunk in 1982 by HMS Conqueror using Mark 8 torpedoes designed in 1925. 323 dead. Captain Bonzo called it "lamentably legal."
- [China Bought a Soviet Aircraft Carrier to Build a Casino. The Casino Never Existed.](https://brvzulu.com/stories/varyag-casino-carrier): A former PLA basketball player bought the unfinished carrier Varyag for $20 million over 50 bottles of baijiu. Macau's harbor was too shallow to dock it. Turkey blocked the Bosphorus. A storm broke the tow lines. Egypt refused the Suez Canal. The ship went around Africa. Nine years later: Liaoning, China's first aircraft carrier. Xu spent $120 million. China said he lacked receipts.

- [The Cruiser That Sank With Five Tons of Soviet Gold and Then Somebody Went and Got It](https://brvzulu.com/stories/hms-edinburgh-gold): HMS Edinburgh was torpedoed three times carrying 465 Soviet gold bars in the Arctic. She sank a German destroyer while crippled. In 1981 divers recovered 431 bars from 800 feet. Five bars are still down there.

- [The Royal Navy Put Steam Engines in Submarines and Lost More Boats to Themselves Than to the Enemy](https://brvzulu.com/stories/k-class-kalamity): 18 steam-powered submarines, zero lost to enemy action, six sank from collisions, fires, and the Battle of May Island where British ships ran over their own sub flotilla. 104 dead in 75 minutes.
- [The Night French Cavalry Rode Across a Frozen Sea and Captured a Fleet](https://brvzulu.com/stories/den-helder-cavalry-fleet): In January 1795, French hussars wrapped their horses' hooves in cloth, rode across a frozen harbor, and boarded 14 Dutch warships. No shots fired. The only time in history cavalry captured a fleet.
- [They Packed a Destroyer With Five Tons of Explosives and Rammed It Into a German Drydock](https://brvzulu.com/stories/hms-campbeltown-st-nazaire): HMS Campbeltown rammed the Normandie Dock at St Nazaire carrying hidden depth charges. They detonated ten hours later while German officers inspected the wreck. The dock didn't reopen until 1950.
- [The Largest Carrier Ever Built Lasted Seventeen Hours](https://brvzulu.com/stories/shinano-maiden-voyage): IJN Shinano, a converted Yamato hull at 72,000 tons, was sunk by USS Archerfish on her maiden voyage. Watertight doors hadn't been installed. Captain Abe went down with her. 1,435 dead.
- [The Navy Ship That's Been Held Hostage Since 1968](https://brvzulu.com/stories/uss-pueblo-captured): USS Pueblo was captured by North Korea with 83 crew. They spent 11 months as prisoners, flipping off cameras and calling it a "Hawaiian good luck sign." The ship is still in Pyongyang. Still commissioned.
- [Praise the Lord and Pass the Ammunition](https://brvzulu.com/stories/uss-new-orleans-pearl-harbor): USS New Orleans was dead at the dock at Pearl Harbor. Chaplain Forgy's shout became a No. 1 song. The ship survived Pearl Harbor, fought at Coral Sea and Midway, lost her bow at Tassafaronga. Three damage control officers died keeping her afloat. The bow was found in 2025.

## Topics Covered

Naval history, warship battles, WWII Pacific theater, WWI naval warfare, commerce raiders, naval engineering failures, ship design flaws, nuclear testing on warships, Cold War naval history, Battle of Tassafaronga, Battle of Penang 1914, Battle of Cherbourg 1864, Operation Crossroads Bikini Atoll, Iron Bottom Sound, treaty cruisers, pocket battleships, Falklands War, Battle of Surigao Strait, K-class submarines, steam-powered submarines, Battle of May Island, St Nazaire Raid, Operation Chariot, French Revolutionary Wars, cavalry vs navy, Den Helder 1795, IJN Shinano, USS Archerfish, USS Pueblo, North Korea, Pearl Harbor, Chaplain Forgy, Praise the Lord and Pass the Ammunition.

## Contact

stories@bravozulu.co
